source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Caught Between Incident During Casing Offload

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Walker Ridge Block 29

actions_taken: Crane operator safely landed the bundle of casing
IP attended to by rig medic
IP evacuated via medevac to hospital in greater New Orleans area
BSEE Houma District office notified orally and written report submitted in eWell within 15 days
BSEE onsite investigation conducted on March 7, 2024
event.summary: On February 16, 2024, during crane operations offloading 16-inch casing from a supply vessel to the pipe deck on the Nabors MODS 400 rig, a deck foreman (IP) was struck by a swinging bundle of casing weighing approximately 11,500 lbs. The IP had turned his back to the suspended load while verifying casing tally and failed to recognize he was positioned in the impact zone between the casing bundle and a sub-rack basket. Despite warnings from riggers, the bundle struck the IP on the right hip, forcing his left hip into the sub-rack basket, resulting in three pelvic fractures. The IP was evacuated to hospital and discharged four days later.
recommendations: BSEE Houma District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time

hazards.name: Suspended Load - 11,500 lbs Bundle of Casing

threats.name: Personnel positioned in impact zone between suspended load and fixed structure
Failure to recognize changing hazard zone as work progresses

consequences.name: Lost time accident requiring medical evacuation

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for Blind Lift
controls.barrier_role: Identify caught between hazard and establish safe work practices
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to identify hazards before work begins

supporting_text: A blind lift Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was in use with General Permit to Work (PTW) and Start-Work Checklist (SWC)
The IP did not follow the JSA that was utilized for the operation
The JSA identified the caught between hazard and recommended not to walk under or between loads while they are suspended
The JSA advised workers to stay 8' from the load while the load is in transit
Human performance error - Not following the hazards and mitigations of the JSA utilized

controls.name: Minimum 8-foot Distance from Suspended Load
controls.barrier_role: Maintain safe separation distance from suspended loads
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to prevent personnel exposure

supporting_text: The JSA advised workers to stay 8' from the load while the load is in transit
The IP did not follow the JSA that was utilized for the operation

controls.name: Prohibition Against Walking Under or Between Suspended Loads
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from entering high-risk zones

supporting_text: The JSA identified the caught between hazard and recommended not to walk under or between loads while they are suspended
He did not realize he was in between the bundle of casing and the sub-rack

controls.name: Taglines and Push-Pull Sticks for Load Control
controls.barrier_role: Control movement and prevent uncontrolled swing of suspended loads
controls.lod_basis: Physical control device to manage load movement

supporting_text: A designated flagger and six riggers handle the load with taglines and push-pull sticks
Riggers use shepherd hooks to grab tag lines
The bundle swings West Northwest and is in line with the joints that were previously laid out
The bundle then swings to the Southeast in the direction of the IP

controls.name: Supervisor Oversight of Lifting Operations
controls.barrier_role: Monitor work execution and ensure compliance with safe work practices

supporting_text: The deck foreman (IP) was supervising the work and verifying the casing tally
It was also discovered that the IP was not only the supervisor of the operation, but he was also responsible for the casing tally
IP was supervising the offloading operations while tending to the casing tally

controls.name: Maintaining Visual Contact with Suspended Load
controls.barrier_role: Maintain situational awareness of load position and movement

supporting_text: The IP turned his back to the load before the casing was set down and wasn't aware that the casing started to move in his direction
IP has his back turned to the load while verifying the casing tally
The IP also turned his back to a suspended load
Human performance error – failure to identify the hazard of the impact zone getting smaller as the job continued. IP turning his back to a suspended load
A rigger yells at the IP to move out of the way
All riggers yell at the IP to move from his position
The IP failed to heed the warning shout from the first rigger that yelled for him to get out of the way
Communication – IP failed to listen to riggers when told to move out of the pinch point

controls.name: Stop Work Authority (SWA)
controls.barrier_role: Empower personnel to halt operations when unsafe conditions are observed
controls.lod_basis: Independent authority to intervene in unsafe operations

supporting_text: Finally, the crane operator and riggers failed to use stop work authority (SWA)
Communication – IP failed to listen to riggers when told to move out of the pinch point. SWA was not used

controls.name: Impact Zone Management - Sub-Rack Positioning
controls.barrier_role: Maintain adequate clearance in work area to prevent pinch points

supporting_text: Upon reviewing pictures, documents, Nabors and Chevron's investigation reports, BSEE concluded that failure to open the impact zone by adjusting the sub rack was the probable cause of the incident
The IP and riggers failed to recognize the hazard presented by the change of the impact zone (area between the casing and sub-rack)
Failure to recognize this hazard by the IP, crane operator, and riggers lead the IP to think he was continuing to work in a safe area
Human performance error – failure to identify the hazard of the impact zone getting smaller as the job continued

controls.name: General Permit to Work (PTW) and Start-Work Checklist (SWC)
controls.barrier_role: Authorize and verify readiness for work execution

supporting_text: A blind lift Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was in use with General Permit to Work (PTW) and Start-Work Checklist (SWC)

controls.name: Designated Flagger for Crane Operations
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate and direct crane movements safely

supporting_text: A designated flagger and six riggers handle the load with taglines and push-pull sticks

controls.name: On-site Medic and Emergency Response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care following injury
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response and medical treatment capability

supporting_text: The IP was attended to by the rig medic
The on-site medic tends to the IP and the decision is made to medivac the IP directly to a hospital

controls.name: Medical Evacuation (Medevac) Capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport injured personnel to advanced medical care

supporting_text: The decision was made to evacuate the IP for transport to the nearest hospital
the decision is made to medivac the IP directly to a hospital in the greater New Orleans area with an ETA of 16:30 hours

controls.name: Crane Operator Safe Landing of Load After Incident
controls.barrier_role: Prevent escalation by safely controlling load after incident

supporting_text: The crane operator safely landed the bundle of casing and the IP was attended to by the rig medic
The crane operator lands the load safely and the IP is attended to

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
